items.headline: Auto Sales Dealership Proposed for El Camino Real
items.summary: Planning Commission is considering a special permit to allow auto sales operations at 975 W. El Camino Real. The existing commercial building would not be expanded or modified externally, though the site would be repaved, restriped, and landscaped.

items.headline: Financial Assistance Pilot Program for Water Customers
items.summary: San José City Council approved a pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers. The program aims to help residents manage water bills, though the specific eligibility criteria and funding level were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: City in legal dispute over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held closed session on May 12 to discuss potential litigation arising from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The council also negotiated regarding properties at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission to consider 27-unit townhome project with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission is scheduled to review a residential project proposing 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. The project will be evaluated for compliance with zoning, design, and affordability requirements.

items.headline: Bloom Energy receives permit for $13M data center expansion
items.summary: Bloom Energy Tower 3 Balance-to-Complete project at 5 Great Oaks Boulevard is cleared to build, representing a $13 million investment in data center infrastructure. This significant commercial expansion reflects continued tech industry presence in San José.

items.headline: City Council to review 72-unit affordable housing project on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: A public hearing is scheduled on the 450 Lytton Avenue project, a six-story building with 72 units of 100% affordable housing on a 0.43-acre city-owned parking lot. The developer is requesting density bonus concessions under state law to make the project feasible.

items.headline: New electrical infrastructure permitted for R&D expansion at Porter Drive
items.summary: Palo Alto has permitted electrical upgrades at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development tenant improvements, including new transformers and conduit extensions. The work signals continued investment in the city's commercial R&D space along the Porter Drive corridor.

items.headline: City Council adopts policy restricting federal civil enforcement on city property
items.summary: Campbell City Council adopted a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and operated property for federal civil enforcement activities. The policy limits access to city facilities for such purposes.
